ocr.text: In the provided table, the experimental analysis spans three time points measured in weeks, specifically 4, 8, and 12 weeks, and
evaluates three different groups: the experimental group, the control group, and the blank control group. For the 4-week time
point, the experimental group shows a value of 0.9890 ±0.0079*#, while the control group demonstrates a value of 0.9651
±0.0075, and the blank control group records a value of 0.9612 ±0.0126.
Experimental group  Control group  Blank control group
At the 8-week mark, the experimental group exhibits a value of 0.9828 ±0.0093*#, in contrast to the control group, which has a
value of 0.9495 ±0.0116#, and the blank control group, which shows a lower value of 0.9317 ±0.0103. By the 12-week interval,
the experimental group reports a value of 0.9579 ±0.0101*#, while the control group reflects a value of 0.9213 ±0.0166#, and
the blank control group reveals a value of 0.8992 ±0.0155. Notably, the asterisks and hashtags indicate statistical significance in
the experimental group at the 4- and 8-week time points, respectively, suggesting a potential efficacy of the treatment under
investigation. The data across all groups and time points highlights a consistent trend where the experimental group maintains
the highest values, followed by the control group and the blank control group, indicating varying responses to the treatment
